title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
SISO? A Smarter Way to Invest in Market Volatility
Introduction
Financial markets are very unpredictable and can prove to be a little difficult to understand for a beginner investor. The sudden rise and fall in prices may leave an investor confused regarding “timing the market” correctly i.e., when to invest in the market, when to hold on, and when to exit. The SISO strategy offered by Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance can solve this issue. Its disciplined approach during investment and redemption phase helps to reduce the impact of volatility without compromising on market-linked returns. Let us understand what SISO is and how it can efficiently handle market swings.
The Challenge of Market Volatility
The financial market never moves in the same pattern. Sometimes the prices might rise quickly while at other times there can be sharp corrections. When the prices go up, many people might rush to buy securities to capitalise on the rising prices. When the financial market falls, investors might exit in panic to avoid losses.
This impulsive buying and selling process is riskier and can prove counterproductive. You might lose out on the returns by trying to time and predict market movements.
What is SISO?
SISO stands for Systematic In, Systematic Out. It is a disciplined investment approach where you make regular, fixed investments into a plan and later withdraw your money in a structured, phased manner. SISO is available in both traditional and unit linked insurance plans, and it can be used to meet various financial goals.
Systematic In means that you pay a fixed amount of premium every month, growing your corpus slowly and steadily.
Systematic Out means that you receive staggered withdrawals at regular intervals, instead of the total maturity benefit in one lump sum amount.
As life insurance coverage is offered in both ULIPs and traditional policies, you not only create a systematic financial plan using SISO, but also receive financial security for your loved ones.
Why SISO is Smarter During Volatility
Let’s understand how SISO reduces the impact of financial market swings with an example:
Smart Entry (Systematic In)
Suppose you invest ₹10,000 in a ULIP every month for 15 years. Let’s say the Net Asset Value (NAV) is ₹100 in the first month. In the first month, after allocating the premium to charges, you are left with ₹9800. This means you can buy 98 units. The next month, when the NAV falls to ₹90, you use the allocated premium of ₹9900 to buy 111 units. So, every dip in the market helps in purchasing more units, while every surge provides fewer units. Over time, the cost is averaged out through the rupee-cost averaging method and you do not have to worry about the right time to enter the financial market.
Smart Exit (Systematic Out)
Suppose you want to start withdrawing some amount from your ULIP from the 11th year. The SISO strategy allows this redemption in parts, let’s say ₹8,000 every month. During this partial withdrawal phase, you get a regular payout irrespective of market movements. The remaining amount stays invested in the financial market and can continue to grow over a long-term horizon. The consistent income source can create a stable inflow which can help you meet your financial obligations.
Key Benefits of SISO in Volatile Markets
Here are some benefits of using the SISO technique to overcome market volatility:
Minimises Risk of Wrong Timing
ULIPs provide market-linked returns, which involve risk. But when SISO is applied to it, you do not have to worry about entering or exiting the financial market at the right time. SISO uses rupee-cost averaging on premium payments and withdrawals, reducing the hassle of timing the market.
Smoothens Market Ups and Downs
SISO balances the highs and lows of the financial market through rupee-cost averaging. Since you put in a fixed amount every month, you will end up buying more units when the cost is low and fewer when the cost rises. In the same way, withdrawing a set amount regularly ensures that the NAV is averaged out and the ups and downs of the financial market do not affect your corpus considerably.
Improved Emotional Comfort
The automatic and structured method in SISO can help you avoid hasty decisions with the ups and downs of financial markets and remain on track.
Cash Flow Flexibility
With SISO, you receive a steady inflow during the redemption period that can help in fulfilling life goals like children’s education or retirement. All the while the remaining fund value stays invested earning market-linked returns.
Discipline
SISO helps in ignoring short-term noise of the financial markets. The habit of regular investments helps in growing your portfolio in a disciplined manner.
Who Should Consider SISO During Volatility?
The SISO strategy of investing in a ULIP is suitable for the following types of individuals:
If you’re planning for future objectives like children’s education, creating a corpus for the future, or saving funds for the golden years of life, SISO can be a good choice. It instils the habit of regular investments.
If you want a predictable income even when the financial market is uncertain, SISO can be your go-to option. You receive a consistent payout, while the rest of your fund is still invested and continues growing.
Conclusion
Financial market volatility cannot be avoided, but how you respond to it can be controlled. With SISO, there is a structured approach during both the accumulation and withdrawal phases, ensuring stability, consistent growth, and peace of mind even in tough times. There is no pressure to time the financial market correctly and you can also enjoy life insurance coverage that comes with the life insurance plans that you choose.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER.
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Bajaj Life Insurance SISO? A Smarter Way to Invest in Market Volatility
Introduction
Financial markets are very unpredictable and can prove to be a little difficult to understand for a beginner investor. The sudden rise and fall in prices may leave an investor confused regarding “timing the market” correctly i.e., when to invest in the market, when to hold on, and when to exit. The SISO strategy offered by Bajaj Life Insurance can solve this issue. Its disciplined approach during investment and redemption phase helps to reduce the impact of volatility without compromising on market-linked returns. Let us understand what SISO is and how it can efficiently handle market swings.
The Challenge of Market Volatility
The financial market never moves in the same pattern. Sometimes the prices might rise quickly while at other times there can be sharp corrections. When the prices go up, many people might rush to buy securities to capitalise on the rising prices. When the financial market falls, investors might exit in panic to avoid losses.
This impulsive buying and selling process is riskier and can prove counterproductive. You might lose out on the returns by trying to time and predict market movements.
What is SISO?
SISO stands for Systematic In, Systematic Out. It is a disciplined investment approach offered by Bajaj Life Insurance. It allows you to average the purchase price through systematic investments, even during market fluctuations, subject to applicable terms and conditions. Here you make regular, fixed investments into a plan and later withdraw your money in a structured, phased manner. However, it is important to note that SISO is available for select plans.
Systematic In means that you pay a fixed amount of premium every month, growing your corpus slowly and steadily.
Systematic Out means that you receive staggered withdrawals at regular intervals, instead of the total maturity benefit in one lump sum amount.
As life insurance coverage is offered in both ULIPs and traditional policies, you not only create a systematic financial plan using SISO, but also receive financial security for your loved ones. But make sure you choose the plans that are available for SISO strategy and understand the associated terms and conditions beforehand.
Why SISO is Smarter During Volatility
Let’s understand how SISO reduces the impact of financial market swings with an example:
Smart Entry (Systematic In)
Suppose you invest ₹10,000 in a ULIP every month for 15 years. Let’s say the Net Asset Value (NAV) is ₹100 in the first month. In the first month, after allocating the premium to charges, you are left with ₹9800. This means you can buy 98 units. The next month, when the NAV falls to ₹90, you use the allocated premium of ₹9900 to buy 111 units. So, every dip in the market helps in purchasing more
units, while every surge provides fewer units. Over time, the cost is averaged out through the rupee- cost averaging method and you do not have to worry about the right time to enter the financial market.
Smart Exit (Systematic Out)
For example: From the 11th policy year onwards, ULIPs allow partial withdrawals through a systematic withdrawal facility. Under this process, you can choose to withdraw a fixed amount at regular intervals—for example, ₹8,000 every month—while keeping the remaining fund value invested. Each withdrawal is made by redeeming units from the fund, and the balance continues to remain invested as per the chosen fund allocation. This enables planned, periodic access to ULIP funds without fully exiting the policy.
Disclaimer: The SISO strategy is available with select Bajaj Life Insurance ULIPs and is subject to applicable terms and conditions.
Key Benefits of SISO in Volatile Markets
Here are some benefits of using the SISO technique to overcome market volatility:
Minimises Risk of Wrong Timing
ULIPs provide market-linked returns, which involve risk. But when SISO is applied to it, you do not have to worry about entering or exiting the financial market at the right time. SISO uses rupee-cost averaging on premium payments and withdrawals, reducing the hassle of timing the market.
Smoothens Market Ups and Downs
SISO balances the highs and lows of the financial market through rupee-cost averaging. Since you put in a fixed amount every month, you will end up buying more units when the cost is low and fewer when the cost rises. In the same way, withdrawing a set amount regularly ensures that the NAV is averaged out and the ups and downs of the financial market do not affect your corpus considerably.
Emotional Stability Amid Market Volatility
The automatic and structured method in SISO can help you avoid hasty decisions with the ups and downs of financial markets and remain on track.
Cash Flow Flexibility
With SISO, you receive a steady inflow during the withdrawal phase that can help in fulfilling life goals like children’s education or retirement. All the while the remaining fund value stays invested earning market-linked returns.
Discipline
SISO helps in ignoring short-term noise of the financial markets. The habit of regular investments helps in growing your portfolio in a disciplined manner.
Who Should Consider SISO During Volatility?
The SISO strategy of investing in a ULIP is suitable for the following types of individuals:
 If you’re planning for future objectives like children’s education, creating a corpus for the future, or saving funds for the golden years of life, SISO can be a good choice. It instils the habit of regular investments.  If you want a predictable income even when the financial market is uncertain, SISO can be your go-to option. You receive a consistent payout, while the rest of your fund is still invested and continues growing.
Conclusion
Financial market volatility cannot be avoided, but how you respond to it can be controlled. With SISO, there is a structured approach during both the accumulation and withdrawal phases, ensuring stability, consistent growth, and peace of mind even in tough times. There is no pressure to time the financial market correctly and you can enjoy life insurance coverage that comes with the life insurance plans that you choose.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER.
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
